text: The gap between the rich and the poor was the widest since the 1920s.
original_text: In 2012 the richest 1 % of the US population earned 22.83 % of the nation 's total pre-tax income resulting in the widest gap between the rich and the poor since the 1920s .

text: After 90 consecutive days of employment or the employee reaches 20 years of age, whichever comes first, the employee must receive the current federal minimum wage or the state minimum wage, whichever is higher.

text: Raising the minimum wage would increase school attendance.

text: Taxpayers pay about $243 billion each year in indirect subsidies to the fast‑food industry because the fast‑food industry pays wages so low that taxpayers must fund public benefits for the fast‑food workers.
original_text: For example in the fast food industry alone research by the University of Illinois and UC Berkeley documents that taxpayers pay about $ 243 billion each year in indirect subsidies to the fast food industry because they pay wages so low that taxpayers must put up $ 243 billion to pay for public benefits for their workers .

text: According to James Sherk , MA , Senior Policy Analyst at the Heritage Foundation , a single mother working full time and earning the federal minimum wage of $ 7.25 an hour would be over $ 260 a month worse off if the minimum wage were raised to $ 10.10 : " While her market income rises by $ 494 , she loses $ 71 in EITC [ earned income tax credit ] refunds , pays $ 37 more in payroll taxes and $ 45 more in state income taxes .

text: If an employer has a tight compensation budget and the minimum wage is raised, the employer can no longer compensate the same number of employees at the higher wage.
original_text: Layoffs : If an employer has a tight compensation budget and the minimum wage is raised , it means they can no longer compensate the same number of employees at a higher rate and must make layoffs to remain within budget .

text: If an employer has a tight compensation budget and the minimum wage is raised, the employer must make layoffs to remain within budget.

text: Employers in low‑cost areas such as Mississippi would need to spend a larger proportion of their revenue to pay minimum‑wage employees than employers in higher‑cost areas such as Hawaii.
original_text: In areas like Mississippi where the cost of living and average incomes are especially low , employers would need to spend proportionally more to pay their minimum wage employees than employers in higher cost areas like Hawaii , and yet would be unable to cover the cost by raising prices because their customers would not be able to afford them .

text: Employers in low‑cost areas would be unable to cover the higher labor cost by raising prices because customers in those areas could not afford higher prices.
original_text: In areas like Mississippi where the cost of living and average incomes are especially low , employers would need to spend proportionally more to pay their minimum wage employees than employers in higher cost areas like Hawaii , and yet would be unable to cover the cost by raising prices because their customers would not be able to afford them .

text: Raising the federal tipped minimum wage ($2.13 per hour since 1991) would not lead to restaurant job losses.

text: A minimum wage job is suppose to be an entry level stepping stone – not a career goal .

text: Seventy percent of US chief financial officers said they would increase contracting, outsourcing, or move production outside the United States if the minimum wage were raised to $10 an hour.
original_text: A 2014 study of 400 US Chief Financial Officers ( CFOs ) by Campbell Harvey , PhD , J. Paul Sticht Professor of International Business at Duke University , found that 70 % of CFOs would " increase contracting , outsourcing , or moving actual production outside the United States " if the minimum wage were raised to $ 10 an hour .
